title: Warm up
content: This slide introduces three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—through illustrated scenes viewed from inside a car. Each image depicts a distinct driving environment representing the city's character, such as traffic and technology in Beijing, mountainous scenery and reading in Chongqing, and a lakeside setting with smart technology in Hangzhou.
key_claims: Beijing is depicted with heavy traffic, skyscrapers, and traditional architecture, suggesting a blend of modernity and tradition.
Chongqing is shown with mountainous terrain and a cable car, hinting at unique geography and tourism.
Hangzhou is illustrated with a lake, smart technology icons, and a robot, suggesting technological innovation.

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This title slide introduces the presentation's theme, 'A Ride Through China,' using images of a taxi sign, the Forbidden City, a nighttime cityscape, and a pagoda. It sets up the narrative framework of exploring China's development through the perspective of a taxi ride across different cities.
key_claims: The presentation is framed as 'A Ride Through China.'
The theme centers on China as an evolving nation.

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the structure of Session 1, which involves investigating and researching city development. It presents a three-step process flow using an illustrated cityscape graphic and color-coded boxes.
key_claims: The research process follows three steps: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact.
Each step flows sequentially into the next, as shown by connecting arrows.

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This slide presents a matching exercise linking three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—to their respective development strategies. Lines connect each city to a strategy, with the correct pairings shown alongside representative images of Chinese architecture and cityscapes.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with Sustainable urban development.
Chongqing is matched with Tourism & Economic growth.
Hangzhou is matched with Technology & tradition integration (融合).

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide summarizes the development strategies of Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou through the perspective of taxi drivers, reiterating the car-view images from the warm-up slide with corrected strategy labels. It concludes with a key takeaway about China's diverse, localized development approach.
key_claims: Beijing's strategy is sustainable urban development.
Chongqing's strategy is tourism & economic growth.
Hangzhou's strategy is technology & tradition integration.
China is an evolving nation with unique development strategies per city.
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote development (因地制宜).
